paper_title: Pliocene Model Intercomparison Project (PlioMIP): experimental design and boundary conditions (Experiment 1)

methods.description: This method involves the adoption and availability of 'preferred' and 'alternate' boundary condition data packages for climate modeling in the PlioMIP project. The preferred package requires changing the model's land/sea mask to a mid-Pliocene configuration, while the alternate package uses a modern configuration to accommodate models that cannot alter the land/sea mask.

steps.description: Both data packages are provided on the PlioMIP website and as supplementary information to this paper.
steps.searchable_summary: download boundary condition data packages from PlioMIP website

steps.description: The preferred data package requires the ability to change the model's land/sea mask to a mid-Pliocene configuration.
steps.searchable_summary: configure model land/sea mask to mid-Pliocene configuration
outputs: configured model with mid-Pliocene land/sea mask

steps.description: The alternate data package, with a modern land/sea configuration, is provided to maximize the potential number of participating groups in PlioMIP.
steps.searchable_summary: use modern land/sea configuration for alternate data package
outputs: configured model with modern land/sea mask

methods.method_name: Implementation of Sea-Surface Temperatures and Topography as Anomalies
methods.description: This method ensures that climate anomalies from all PlioMIP climate models are directly comparable by implementing Pliocene topography and SSTs as anomalies to the standard modern datasets used by each modeling group.

steps.description: Calculate the difference between the PRISM Pliocene and PRISM Modern topography/SST and add it to the modern SST and topographic datasets used by each modeling group.
Use absolute Pliocene topography/SST in regions where no modern data is given to overcome potential mismatches.
steps.searchable_summary: apply absolute Pliocene data where modern data is missing

steps.description: Project modern SST on the same Pliocene grids to make anomalies easier to generate.
steps.searchable_summary: project modern SST onto Pliocene grids

methods.method_name: Data Processing and Storage for PlioMIP
methods.description: This method outlines the data processing, storage, and analysis requirements for PlioMIP Phase 1, including the adoption of PMIP2 variables, netCDF format, and the use of the CMOR library.

steps.description: Prepare data files in netCDF format conforming to the CF metadata convention.
steps.searchable_summary: convert data files to netCDF format with CF metadata

steps.description: Ensure there is only one output variable per file.
Use regular grids for data that are a function of longitude and latitude.
steps.searchable_summary: apply regular grids to geospatial data

steps.description: Follow the PMIP2 file name convention to ensure unique file names.
steps.searchable_summary: rename files according to PMIP2 convention

steps.description: Use the CMOR library to create files for submission to the database.
steps.searchable_summary: use CMOR library for data file creation
